conbody.p: Pneumatic tyres must be inflated to the correct pressure. Incorrect tyre pressure can cause instability and affect the load capacity of the lift truck.
Front Tyre Inflation Pressure – CD100S-7: 9.00-20 tyres at 930 kPa (135 psi) | CD120S-7: 10.00-20 tyres at 860 kPa (125 psi)
Front Tyre Inflation Pressure – CD140S-7: 12.00-20 tyres at 790 kPa (115 psi) | CD160S-7: 14.00-20 tyres at 720 kPa (105 psi)
Rear Tyre Inflation Pressure – All models: Refer to tyre placard on vehicle for correct rear tyre pressures.
conbody.note: Never inflate tyres beyond the recommended pressure. Always use a tyre inflation cage when inflating tyres. Stand to the side of the tyre, never in front.
conbody.p: Check tyre pressure daily before operation. Tyres that are significantly underinflated must not be operated until properly inflated.
Page 468 of 500 | CD100S-7 / CD120S-7 / CD140S-7 / CD160S-7 Operation & Maintenance Manual | DL06P Tier-4
Always follow all safety precautions when operating or maintaining this equipment.
Ensure all operators are properly trained and certified before operating the lift truck.
Regular maintenance is essential to safe and efficient operation of the lift truck.
Contact your local CROWN dealer for parts, service, and technical support.
Keep this manual with the lift truck at all times for reference by operators and technicians.
Record all service and maintenance activities in the maintenance logbook.
Only use genuine CROWN replacement parts to maintain warranty and performance.
